DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-01-14 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Arthur Plunk & Henrietta Plunk
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / opposing party RPI

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE
Hearing held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling issued on August 6, 2014 as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is December 1, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day under C.C.P. 36(f) will be dictated by the noticed hearing date for this motion and is December 5, 2014. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 25, 2014. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P., unless plaintiffs stipulate otherwise. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendant no later than September 5, 2014. Response to the demand is due no later than September 12, 2014. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating the best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by November 21, 2014 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on November 25, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 14, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 26, 2014. No later than August 29, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than August 29, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on September 24, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on December 1, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by November 26, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Plaintiff will meet and confer with RPI regarding notice of hearing on motion for summary judgment should plaintiff's deposition not concluded on 8/22/14. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Continued To Aug-28-14 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503

Defendant Rpi Companys Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff Arthur Plunk To Appear And Testify At Deposition
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo E. Sandoval and Jeffrey M. Gebhardt of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party RPI Company

RULING:
DEFENDANT RPI COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF ARTHUR PLUNK TO APPEAR AND TESTIFY AT DEPOSITION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant RPI Company's motion to compel plaintiff Arthur Plunk to appear and testify at deposition is granted. As agreed upon by the parties, the scope of the deposition is limited to plaintiff's medical condition and damages. Mr. Plunk shall appear for an in-person deposition. As agreed to by defendant, such deposition shall occur at a location convenient to plaintiff. No time limit is imposed. Prevailing party to prepare an order for the Court's signature. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
